official_baseline.name: FAISS + Matching V3 normalized
official_baseline.reason: This baseline combines FAISS semantic retrieval with explicit Matching V3 scoring, must-have coverage, profile quality, and risk penalties.

compared_runs.name: CrossEncoder reranking constrained by must-have coverage

baseline_summary.method: FAISS retrieval + Matching V3 normalized scoring
baseline_summary.interpretation: The baseline keeps the strongest business-compatible candidates at the top while still exposing lower-coverage candidates later in the list with explicit coverage signals.

unconstrained_cross_encoder_summary.method: FAISS top20 followed by CrossEncoder reranking without business filter
unconstrained_cross_encoder_summary.main_issue: The unconstrained CrossEncoder is semantically interesting but unsafe as a main engine because it promotes low must-have coverage candidates.

constrained_cross_encoder_summary.method: FAISS top20 filtered by must_have_coverage >= 0.6, then CrossEncoder reranking
constrained_cross_encoder_summary.validation_criterion: No candidate with must_have_coverage < 0.6 appears in reranked_top_10.
constrained_cross_encoder_summary.main_effect: The constrained run blocks low-coverage candidates such as JEFFERYGORCZANY and Trisha Stamm before CrossEncoder reranking.

key_observations: CrossEncoder alone can reward semantic similarity even when must-have skill coverage is weak.
The unconstrained run promoted JEFFERYGORCZANY to rank 1 despite must_have_coverage = 0.4.
The unconstrained run allowed Trisha Stamm into the top 10 despite must_have_coverage = 0.2.
The constrained run prevents candidates below 0.6 coverage from entering the CrossEncoder reranking stage.
The constrained run is safer, but only 3 of 20 FAISS candidates passed the filter, which makes it too restrictive as a final production ranking strategy.
risks: Using unconstrained CrossEncoder as the main ranking engine can produce business-invalid recommendations.
Using the constrained CrossEncoder as the final engine may over-filter the pool and reduce recruiter choice.
The current evidence is based on one demo job profile, so generalization across roles is not proven.
CrossEncoder latency is acceptable for experimentation, but should be evaluated on larger candidate pools before productization.

decision.official_baseline_decision: FAISS + Matching V3 normalized reste la baseline officielle.
decision.unconstrained_cross_encoder_decision: CrossEncoder non contraint est rejete comme moteur principal.
decision.constrained_cross_encoder_decision: CrossEncoder contraint est valide comme experience controlee, mais reste experimental.
decision.main_reason: Il corrige la remontee de candidats faibles, mais seulement 3 candidats sur 20 passent le filtre, donc il est trop restrictif comme moteur final.

recommended_next_step.short_term: Keep FAISS + Matching V3 normalized as the official demo and evaluation baseline.
recommended_next_step.experimental_next_step: Test a hybrid strategy where must-have coverage remains a hard guardrail and CrossEncoder is used only as a secondary tie-breaker among admissible candidates.
recommended_next_step.future_work: Evaluate several job profiles and thresholds before deciding whether CrossEncoder belongs in the final matching module.

main_demo: Show FAISS + Matching V3 normalized as the official baseline.
Explain the score using semantic similarity, must-have coverage, matched skills, missing skills, and profile quality.
Use Hichem Bensalah, Candidate_8eea1b635447, and MOHAMED AZIZ BELAWEID as the main top candidates.
experimentation_only: Mention the unconstrained CrossEncoder only as an experiment that exposed a risk.
Mention the constrained CrossEncoder as a controlled experiment that fixes the low-coverage promotion issue.
Use this ablation to show that the project made a justified architecture decision rather than blindly adding a reranker.
do_not_present_as_baseline: Do not present CrossEncoder non contraint as the final matching engine.
Do not present CrossEncoder contraint as production-ready or as the official baseline.
Do not claim that the constrained CrossEncoder is superior overall; it is safer but too restrictive on the current job.
